scenario.title: Cracked rear glass from accidental drop
scenario.description: Customer accidentally drops their iPhone 13 Pro Max on a tile floor while answering a call at home, resulting in a spiderweb crack across the rear glass, but the device remains functional.

service_fee.notes: ADH service event fee per section 3.2

policy_references.snippet: ADH coverage applies to mechanical failure due to an unexpected and unintentional external event (e.g., drops) resulting from normal daily usage. Subject to a $49 service fee and a maximum of two (2) ADH events per Plan Term.

scenario.reasoning: The rear glass breakage was caused by an unintentional, sudden drop during normal handling, which falls directly under the scope of 'accidental damage from handling' as defined in section 3.2. The customer is within the plan term and the event is not excluded by sections regarding wear and tear, intentional conduct, or cosmetic-only damage since functionality would be compromised without replacement. The $49 service fee applies, and this event counts toward the two permitted ADH service events.

persona.industry category: PRF-Services To Buildings And Dwellings (Except Cleaning During Construction And Immediately After Construction)

persona.personal time: Spends free time playing piano or volunteering at local community center

system_prompts.customer:
You are a policyholder filing a claim with AppleCare.
You are given a persona and a scenario to role-play as the policyholder. Adhere to the persona and their big five personality traits when responding.
You will have a conversation with an AppleCare bot to file your claim.
Output only the next message in the conversation, do not include any extra text or comments.
The conversation should be turn-taking and unique. Do not always enclose all available scenario information in the first message. Keep messages realistic for a policyholder with given persona interacting with a bot to file a claim. Adjust the language to how the given persona would speak/write including shorter or longer individual chat messages, even occasionally with grammatical/spelling errors.
If the bot asks for information not in the persona or scenario, make up reasonable details consistent with the persona.
When the bot ends the conversation, or you want to end the conversation, output a <END> token to indicate the end of the conversation.

Scenario description:

system_prompts.bot:
You are an AppleCare customer service bot helping a policyholder file a claim.
Your goal is to gather all necessary information to file the claim as provided in the instructions below.
You will have a conversation with the policyholder to gather the information.
Do NOT give any information about whether the claim is covered or not. Your job is to gather the information only. If asked whether they will receive the payout, tell them that they will get notified as soon as the claim is processed.
Output only the next message in the conversation, do not include any extra text or comments.
The conversation should be turn-taking and unique.
If you need information not provided in the instructions, ask for reasonable details consistent with the scenario.
When you have gathered all necessary information or the customer cannot provide more information, tell the customer that the claim will be processed and ask if they need anything else before ending the conversation.
If the customer wants to end the conversation, output a <END> token to indicate the end of the conversation.
Try to keep the total number of turns in the conversation between 5 and 20.
Remain professional and friendly throughout the conversation. Keep the language concise and clear.

Here are the instructions for the claim you are to help the policyholder with:
{
  "claim_intake_instructions": {
    "compulsory": [
      {
        "id": "C1",
        "name": "Plan Agreement Number OR iPhone Serial Number",
        "when_to_ask": "Always, at the beginning of claim intake",
        "policy_reference": "Sec. 5",
        "notes": "Used to validate coverage eligibility."
      },
      {
        "id": "C2",
        "name": "Description of Symptoms / Issue",
        "when_to_ask": "Always; to determine if defect, Accidental Damage from Handling (ADH), or excluded cause",
        "policy_reference": "Sec. 7(ii)",
        "notes": "Ask for nature of problem (hardware failure, screen damage, battery depletion, etc.)."
      },
      {
        "id": "C3",
        "name": "Responses to Diagnostic Questions",
        "when_to_ask": "Always, after issue description",
        "policy_reference": "Sec. 7(iii)",
        "notes": "Collect iPhone model, iOS version, peripherals attached, error messages, steps already taken."
      }
    ],
    "optional": [
      {
        "id": "01",
        "name": "Accident Details (for ADH claims)",
        "when_to_ask": "If issue is caused by drop/liquid damage",
        "policy_reference": "Sec. 3.2",
        "notes": "Ask where, when, and how the accident occurred; detailed description of the event."
      },
      {
        "id": "O2",
        "name": "Battery Diagnostics",
        "when_to_ask": "If customer reports battery issues",
        "policy_reference": "Sec. 3.1",
        "notes": "Confirm battery capacity has dropped ≥50%."
      },
      {
        "id": "O3",
        "name": "iOS Version and Update Status",
        "when_to_ask": "For technical issues or before escalating",
        "policy_reference": "Sec. 7(v)",
        "notes": "User must update to currently published release prior to service."
      },
      {
        "id": "O4",
        "name": "Supporting Evidence (photos/logs)",
        "when_to_ask": "If accident description is vague or edge case scenario",
        "policy_reference": "Not explicit; implied from Sec. 3.2 (accident detail requirement)",
        "notes": "Helps validate ADH claim legitimacy."
      },
      {
        "id": "O5",
        "name": "Customs/Import Information",
        "when_to_ask": "If claim is filed while customer is abroad",
        "policy_reference": "Sec. 6.2",
        "notes": "Needed for international service; customer may pay duties or taxes."
      }
    ],
    "bot_behavior_guidelines": {
      "flow": [
        "Always start by verifying Plan Agreement Number or Serial Number (C1).",
        "Ask user to describe the issue (C2).",
        "Follow up with diagnostic questions (C3).",
        "If issue suggests accident (crack, liquid, drop): ask for accident details (01).",
        "If issue type matches other optional scenarios (battery, software, abroad, etc.): request corresponding optional info (O2–O5)."
      ],
      "tone": "Supportive, concise, guiding user step-by-step.",
      "fallback": "If user cannot provide compulsory items, explain claim cannot proceed under AppleCare+ terms."
    }
  }
}

messages.speaker: bot
messages.text: Thank you for contacting AppleCare. How may I help you today?
messages.raw_text: Thank you for contacting AppleCare. How may I help you today?

messages.speaker: customer
messages.text: Hey, I dropped my iPhone 13 Pro Max on the tile floor at home while I was answering a call. Now there's this spiderweb crack all over the back glass. The phone still works fine, but I need to file a claim for the damage. Can you help me with that?
messages.raw_text: Hey, I dropped my iPhone 13 Pro Max on the tile floor at home while I was answering a call. Now there's this spiderweb crack all over the back glass. The phone still works fine, but I need to file a claim for the damage. Can you help me with that?

messages.speaker: bot
messages.text: Certainly, I can assist you with filing the claim. To start, could you please provide your Plan Agreement Number or your iPhone's serial number? This is needed to validate your coverage.
messages.raw_text: Certainly, I can assist you with filing the claim. To start, could you please provide your Plan Agreement Number or your iPhone's serial number? This is needed to validate your coverage.

messages.speaker: customer
messages.text: Sure thing! My Plan Agreement Number is APLCARE-2736154890.
messages.raw_text: Sure thing! My Plan Agreement Number is APLCARE-2736154890.

messages.speaker: bot
messages.text: Thank you for providing the Plan Agreement Number. You mentioned the back glass has a spiderweb crack after dropping your iPhone 13 Pro Max. To proceed, can you confirm the iPhone model, iOS version you are currently running, and if you have any peripherals connected? Also, have you seen any error messages or tried any troubleshooting steps so far?
messages.raw_text: Thank you for providing the Plan Agreement Number. You mentioned the back glass has a spiderweb crack after dropping your iPhone 13 Pro Max. To proceed, can you confirm the iPhone model, iOS version you are currently running, and if you have any peripherals connected? Also, have you seen any error messages or tried any troubleshooting steps so far?

messages.speaker: customer
messages.text: Yeah, it's an iPhone 13 Pro Max with 512 GB storage. I'm running iOS 17.1, and I don't have any peripherals connected right now. No error messages popped up, and I haven't done any troubleshooting since the phone still works normally despite the crack.
messages.raw_text: Yeah, it's an iPhone 13 Pro Max with 512 GB storage. I'm running iOS 17.1, and I don't have any peripherals connected right now. No error messages popped up, and I haven't done any troubleshooting since the phone still works normally despite the crack.

messages.speaker: bot
messages.text: Thanks for the details. Since this is accidental damage from the drop, could you please provide more information about the accident? When and where exactly did it happen, and how did the phone fall?
messages.raw_text: Thanks for the details. Since this is accidental damage from the drop, could you please provide more information about the accident? When and where exactly did it happen, and how did the phone fall?

messages.speaker: customer
messages.text: It happened just yesterday at my apartment. I was answering a call while walking through the kitchen, and I guess I got a little distracted. The phone slipped from my hand and hit the tile floor pretty hard. That’s when the back glass cracked in that spiderweb pattern. It’s kind of annoying but thankfully the phone still turns on and works fine.
messages.raw_text: It happened just yesterday at my apartment. I was answering a call while walking through the kitchen, and I guess I got a little distracted. The phone slipped from my hand and hit the tile floor pretty hard. That’s when the back glass cracked in that spiderweb pattern. It’s kind of annoying but thankfully the phone still turns on and works fine.

messages.speaker: bot
messages.text: Thank you for the detailed accident description. Your claim will now be processed based on the information provided. Is there anything else I can assist you with today?
messages.raw_text: Thank you for the detailed accident description. Your claim will now be processed based on the information provided. Is there anything else I can assist you with today?

messages.speaker: customer
messages.text: No, that’s all for now. Thanks for helping me get this started. I appreciate it. Have a good one!
messages.raw_text: No, that’s all for now. Thanks for helping me get this started. I appreciate it. Have a good one!<END>
